data.title: As a plugin maintainer, I can see code review outcomes in the event log
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Instrument REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md to emit review_result events after each code review
**Why**: Code review is the most frequent review type — instrumenting it first proves the emit-store pipeline end-to-end
**Scope**:
- IN: Emit events with tier (light/standard/deep), pass/fail, dimension scores, finding counts by severity and dimension, resolution actions (code-change vs defense count), overlay trigger status (security, performance), tier escalation data (original -> final), test_gate_status field (passed/not_passed/unknown from test-gate-status artifact)
- OUT: Plan review, fidelity review, commit workflow instrumentation

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md instructs agents to emit review_result events with all specified fields after each code review
  <- Satisfies: 'Review workflows emit structured JSONL events'
- When this story is complete, events include a test_gate_status field derived from the test-gate-status artifact at event emission time
  <- Satisfies: 'test suite was green at time of review' (SC3 data source)
- When this story is complete, unit tests verify event emission with fixture review data
  <- Satisfies: unit test DoD

## Considerations

- [Scope] Event emission must happen in the orchestrator (REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md) not inside 10+ individual agent files — tier, escalation, and overlay data is only available at orchestrator level
- [Testing] Event emission is prose-instructed — tests must verify events were written
- [Reliability] Push bug 7895-e2a5 may prevent remote persistence; local writes must work independently
